summary: Fair audience‑sourced recording with occasional drops, but an energetic show featuring strong Jerry vocals and a standout Terrapin Station jam.
review: The performance is lively – Jerry’s voice is powerful, the band plays with more discipline than usual, and the post‑Terrapin jam shines. Recording quality is mixed: a generally tolerable audience capture suffers from a two‑second power loss on “Eyes” and occasional muffled pockets‑recorder sound. Listeners who value playing will enjoy it despite the technical flaws.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality is moderate to good (energetic, strong vocals, notable Terrapin jam) while recording quality ranges from tolerable to poor, pulling the overall rating toward the middle of the scale.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally tolerable audience capture; noticeable 2‑second power drop in “Eyes,” low mix of Wince, occasional muffled pocket‑recorder sound. Some reviewers praise a clear rig setup.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The band plays with tighter discipline than earlier shows, limiting free‑form excursions but delivering an energetic set; the jam after Terrapin Station stands out as a high point.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Vocals strong and commanding with plenty of ripping guitar runs.
band_member_comments.Keys: Reviewers hear more keyboard presence than expected, suggesting a solid mix for the keys.
